42 macro so today's key macro question
is what will derail the year end
performance chase our answers are while
there are several catalysts this week
that have the potential disrupt bullish
price momentum we do not view lasting
disruption as the base case for asset
markets for trending for a trending
risk-off Market regime to develop one or
more of the following outcomes must come
true number one our 28-month old
resilient US economy theme must be
disproven by preponderance of key
economic data number two incremental
evidence of our 36-month old sticky
inflation theme must catalyze a change
of heart at the fed and truncate our
14-month old J1 to soft Landing theme
Andor number three the size sequence and
scope of presid president Trump's policy
agenda must evolve in a way that
transforms our recently introduced trip
s's theme from a potential bearish
Catalyst for asset markets to an actual
bearish Catalyst while the macro and
policy calendar this week is somewhat
robust we do not believe investors will
learn enough bad news uh uh to to for
one or more of those AFF forementioned
scenarios to become true among the minds
of investor consensus transition to our

up with the question from our community

next year what impact do you think that
could have on the real economy and on

you're talking about uh so a global
financing air pocket you're talking
about you know the ex anti yield on
asset markets rising to create balance
sheet space for uh you know for big
refinancing ball and so ultimately what
that ultimately means is you have to
have asset price valuations down you
need to have bond yields higher uh in
the credit markets and so that clearly
would create some some negative
performance in asset markets and it's
our belief that the US economy being the
topheavy k-shaped economy that it is
anything that causes negativity in asset
markets that persists for an extended
period of time is going to slow economic
activity here in the US economy and by
extension the global economy so that's
the kind of impact that it would likely
have on the real economy and with
respect to treasury race specifically if
we were in the preco slow flation period
uh you know which sort of generally
persisted post GFC or if we were in this
sort of persistent downtrending
inflation regime which you know has been
in existence since the early 1980s then
you would expect to see uh uh that that
type of liquidity a refinancing air
pocket uh cause lower bond yields you
know but in this sort of postco uhe
regime in which a lot of the fourth
turning dynamics that we've been
highlighting in our research have become
increasingly true in an accelerating
fashion uh it's very likely that we have
um fiscal dominance create higher uh
interest rates uh in this in any sort of
refinancing air pocket so it just really
depends on where you are with respect to
the inflation regime uh the fiscal
regime if there's you know too much
government or not enough government you
can make the case there wasn't enough
